message:
fix: display mail timestamps in local timezone instead of UTC

All gt mail display commands (inbox, read, thread, channel, announce,
queue, search) now call .Local() before formatting timestamps, so times
render in the user local timezone (e.g. MST) instead of raw UTC.

Cherry-picked from PR #2482.

Co-authored-by: Chris Deal <DreadPirateRobertz@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -149,7 +149,7 @@ func readAnnounceChannel(townRoot string, cfg *config.MessagingConfig, channelNa

-			style.Dim.Render(msg.Created.Format("2006-01-02 15:04")))
+			style.Dim.Render(msg.Created.Local().Format("2006-01-02 15:04")))
 		if msg.Description != "" {
 			// Show first line of description as preview
 			lines := strings.SplitN(msg.Description, "\n", 2)
